# UC-15.3.7 · Fire Suppression and Detection System Alarms

## Description

Fire and gas detection events require immediate response. Centralized alarm monitoring ensures rapid escalation and audit trail.

## Value

Fire and gas detection events require immediate response. Centralized alarm monitoring ensures rapid escalation and audit trail.

## Implementation

Integrate fire panel or BMS for alarm events. Alert immediately on any alarm or suppression activation. Log all events for compliance. Report on alarm history and trouble events.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Fire alarm panel integration, BMS.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Fire detection, suppression system status, alarm events.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Integrate fire panel or BMS for alarm events. Alert immediately on any alarm or suppression activation. Log all events for compliance. Report on alarm history and trouble events.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="fire:alarm"
| search (status="alarm" OR status="trouble" OR type="suppression")
| table _time, zone, type, status, description
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Fire Suppression and Detection System Alarms** — Fire and gas detection events require immediate response. Centralized alarm monitoring ensures rapid escalation and audit trail.

Documented **Data sources**: Fire detection, suppression system status, alarm events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Fire alarm panel integration, BMS. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: fire:alarm. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="fire:alarm". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Pipeline stage (see **Fire Suppression and Detection System Alarms**): table _time, zone, type, status, description
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the BMS console for the same equipment, sensors, and time range.

Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (alarms), Timeline (events), Single value (active alarms).

## SPL

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="fire:alarm"
| search (status="alarm" OR status="trouble" OR type="suppression")
| table _time, zone, type, status, description
| sort -_time
```
